chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الرَّجُلِ يَبْعَثُ بِالْهَدْيِ تَطَوُّعاً وَ يُقِيمُ فِي أَهْلِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 211 – The man sends the sacrificial animal voluntarily, and he stays among his family

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/243)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/243)
text: إِنَّ مُرَاداً بَعَثَ بِبَدَنَةٍ وَ أَمَرَ أَنْ تُقَلَّدَ وَ تُشْعَرَ فِي يَوْمِ كَذَا وَ كَذَا فَقُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّمَا يَنْبَغِي أَنْ لَا يَلْبَسَ الثِّيَابَ فَبَعَثَنِي إِلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) بِالْحِيرَةِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ مُرَاداً صَنَعَ كَذَا وَ كَذَا وَ إِنَّهُ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يَتْرُكَ الثِّيَابَ لِمَكَانِ زِيَادٍ فَقَالَ مُرْهُ أَنْ يَلْبَسَ الثِّيَابَ وَ لْيَذْبَحْ بَقَرَةً يَوْمَ الْأَضْحَى عَنْ نَفْسِهِ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Haroun Bin Kharjat who said,

‘Murada sent his sacrificial animal and instructed that it be yoked and marked on such and such a day. So I said to him, ‘But rather, it is befitting that you do not wear the (normal) clothes’. So he sent me to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> at Al-Hira, and I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Murada did such and such, and he did not have the capacity that he neglects the (normal) clothing due to Ziyad’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Instruct him that he should wear the (normal) clothes, and let him slaughter a cow on the day of the slaughtering from himself’.
en.sarwar: 4. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan ibn Yahya from Flarun ibn Kharijah who has said the following: “Murad once sent a badanah as offering and commanded to mark and tag it on such and such day. I then said that it is proper not to wear clothes. He then sent me to ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, in al- Hirah, (a place in Iraq). I said to him (the Imam) that Murad has done such and such things and he is not able to ignore the clothes because of Ziyad. He (the Imam) said, ‘Instruct him to wear the clothes and slaughter a cow on the day of al-Adha’ for himself.’” NOTE:(Fatwa best explains Hadith 4)
